title: Methods and processes to enable rnn-gnn-based network digital twin for o-ran
abstract: One example method includes using a network digital twin of a communications network to perform operations including monitoring, on an ongoing basis, a status of the communications network as the communication network changes, maintaining synchronization between the communications network and the network digital twin based on changes to the status, and the status changes are determined using information gathered during the monitoring, and using the network status to make a prediction regarding performance of the communications network.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method, comprising: with a network digital twin of a communications network, performing operations comprising: monitoring, on an ongoing basis, a status of the communications network as the communication network changes; maintaining synchronization between the communications network and the network digital twin based on changes to the status, and the status changes are determined using information gathered during the monitoring; and using the network status to make a prediction regarding performance of the communications network. 2. The method as recited in claim 1, wherein a recurrent neural network of the network digital twin captures temporal dynamics of the communications network, and the temporal dynamics are used to make the prediction. 3. The method as recited in claim 1, wherein a graph neural network of the network digital twin captures information about spatial relationships and connectivity between components of the communications network, and the information is used to make the prediction. 4. The method as recited in claim 1, wherein the prediction comprises a key performance indicator of the communications network. 5. The method as recited in claim 1, wherein the communications network comprises an open radio access network. 6. The method as recited in claim 1, wherein the prediction comprises a key performance indicator of the communications network, and the key performance indicator is used as a basis for adjusting the performance of the communications network. 7. The method as recited in claim 1, wherein the network digital twin comprises information about relationships between nodes of the communications network that were modeled with a probabilistic graph model. 8. The method as recited in claim 1, wherein the network digital twin models temporal aspects, and spatial aspects, of the communications network. 9. The method as recited in claim 1, wherein the prediction is made in real time while the communications network is operating. 10. The method as recited in claim 1, wherein the network digital twin models connections between nodes of the communications network as variable connections. 11. A non-transitory storage medium having stored therein instructions that are executable by one or more hardware processors to: perform, with a network digital twin of a communications network, operations comprising: monitoring, on an ongoing basis, a status of the communications network as the communication network changes; maintaining synchronization between the communications network and the network digital twin based on changes to the status, and the status changes are determined using information gathered during the monitoring; and using the network status to make a prediction regarding performance of the communications network. 12. The non-transitory storage medium as recited in claim 11, wherein a recurrent neural network of the network digital twin captures temporal dynamics of the communications network, and the temporal dynamics are used to make the prediction. 13. The non-transitory storage medium as recited in claim 11, wherein a graph neural network of the network digital twin captures information about spatial relationships and connectivity between components of the communications network, and the information is used to make the prediction. 14. The non-transitory storage medium as recited in claim 11, wherein the prediction comprises a key performance indicator of the communications network. 15. The non-transitory storage medium as recited in claim 11, wherein the communications network comprises an open radio access network. 16. The non-transitory storage medium as recited in claim 11, wherein the prediction comprises a key performance indicator of the communications network, and the key performance indicator is used as a basis for adjusting the performance of the communications network. 17. The non-transitory storage medium as recited in claim 11, wherein the 

network digital twin comprises information about relationships between nodes of the communications network that were modeled with a probabilistic graph model. 18. The non-transitory storage medium as recited in claim 11, wherein the network digital twin models temporal aspects, and spatial aspects, of the communications network. 19. The non-transitory storage medium as recited in claim 11, wherein the prediction is made in real time while the communications network is operating. 20. The non-transitory storage medium as recited in claim 11, wherein the network digital twin models connections between nodes of the communications network as variable connections.
